# Pull Request Template

## 📋 Description
<!-- Provide a brief description of the changes in this PR -->

## 🔗 Related Issues
<!-- Link to any related issues -->
- Closes #
- Fixes #
- Related to #

## 🚀 Type of Change
<!-- Mark with an `x` all that apply -->
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🎨 Code style update (formatting, renaming)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] 🧪 Test update
- [ ] 🔧 Build/CI update

## 🧪 Testing
<!-- Describe the tests that you ran to verify your changes -->
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Backend API tests pass (if applicable)
- [ ] Frontend E2E tests pass (if applicable)

**Test Configuration:**
- Browser(s): 
- Node.js version: 
- Python version (backend): 

## 📱 Screenshots (if applicable)
<!-- Add screenshots to help explain your changes -->

## ✅ Checklist
<!-- Mark with an `x` all that apply -->
- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published

## 🔍 Additional Notes
<!-- Any additional information, concerns, or context -->

## 📋 Deployment Notes
<!-- Any special deployment considerations -->
- [ ] Database migrations required
- [ ] Environment variables updated
- [ ] API changes require frontend updates
- [ ] Documentation updates required

---

**Branch Strategy:**
- Feature branches merge to `develop`
- Hotfixes can merge to `main` and `develop`
- Release branches merge to `main` and `develop`
